meta.title: Tender Flow workflow map
meta.description: Interactive architecture map for app workflows between packages, components, desktop services, server helpers, and external systems.

packages.label: User / browser
packages.responsibility: Loads React app and starts user actions in web or Electron renderer.
packages.security: No privileged desktop APIs are available unless routed through preload guards.

packages.label: AppShell
packages.responsibility: Top-level composition for providers, app content, help overlay, toasts, cookie consent, and SEO.
packages.security: Keeps cross-cutting providers centralized so auth, help, toast, and SEO behavior is consistent.

packages.label: AppProviders + contexts
packages.responsibility: Provides auth session, feature capability state, UI modals, and React Query wiring.
packages.security: FeatureContext and AuthContext fail closed when subscription/auth data is unavailable.

packages.label: Router + feature registry
packages.responsibility: Parses URLs, navigates internally, and lazy-loads feature modules from manifests.
packages.security: Route helpers avoid ad-hoc URL construction; external links use noopener/noreferrer.

packages.label: MainLayout + navigation
packages.responsibility: App chrome, side navigation, selected project routing, account menu, and desktop drag-safe headers.
packages.security: Navigation is driven by current view and feature availability, not direct DOM state.

packages.label: Command Center
packages.responsibility: Operational dashboard, derived actions, calendar/feed/KPI modules, and task quick actions.
packages.security: Aggregates already-authorized app data instead of opening new data paths.

packages.label: Projects feature
packages.responsibility: Project management, project detail tabs, overview, schedule, tender plan, documents, and contracts.
packages.security: Uses project APIs and RLS-backed services; clone/export flows keep ownership explicit.

packages.label: Pipeline UI components
packages.responsibility: Bid cards, categories, subcontractor selection, pipeline navigation, and bid communication state.
packages.security: Contact and bid updates are mediated by typed actions rather than direct persistence from cards.

packages.label: Contacts feature
packages.responsibility: Subcontractor/contact list, filters, bulk edits, cards, and import wizard.
packages.security: Import parsing normalizes rows before merge and service persistence.

packages.label: Settings feature
packages.responsibility: Profile, notifications, tools, billing, organization settings, admin panels, AI settings, and desktop-only tools.
packages.security: Admin-only panels and sensitive tools use guards and server-side/RPC authorization.

packages.label: Organization UI
packages.responsibility: Organization overview, member management, join requests, billing, branding, and seat usage.
packages.security: Owner/admin checks, seat limits, and RPC authorization protect membership changes.

packages.label: Subscription + billing UI
packages.responsibility: User and organization subscription views, plan changes, wallet/billing actions, and feature visibility.
packages.security: Client shows state but billing mutations depend on RPC/action authorization.

packages.label: Notifications + help
packages.responsibility: Toast notifications, notification center, contextual help overlay, and release notes modal.
packages.security: User-facing errors avoid leaking raw secrets and rely on sanitized messages.

packages.label: Voice assistant
packages.responsibility: Desktop/admin-only assistant launcher, panel, tool gateway, realtime session, and text response APIs.
packages.security: Route availability and admin/desktop checks constrain when assistant tools are enabled.

packages.label: Shared domain + UI
packages.responsibility: Cross-feature types, route helpers, security helpers, formatting, UI primitives, config, and constants.
packages.security: Central helpers include redirect validation, log sanitization, and safer markdown rendering.

packages.label: Hooks + React Query
packages.responsibility: Client data loading, optimistic mutations, project/contact query keys, desktop state, theme, and local storage.
packages.security: Query client handles auth-sensitive failures and invalidation through shared patterns.

packages.label: Legacy services
packages.responsibility: Supabase access, project/contact/document/email/export services, feature usage, URL shortener, AI, and organization APIs.
packages.security: Services are the main boundary for validation, RPC naming, and log-safe error handling.

packages.label: Infra adapters
packages.responsibility: Newer adapter layer for auth, billing, db, files, functions, diagnostics, desktop, export, and projects.
packages.security: Keeps platform-specific or privileged behavior out of feature UI modules.

packages.label: Supabase + Edge Functions
packages.responsibility: Auth, database tables, Storage, RPC functions, Edge Functions, RLS enforcement, and realtime session state.
packages.security: RLS, RPC authorization, service role isolation, and sanitized client access are critical controls.

packages.label: External services
packages.responsibility: Third-party APIs or platform tooling used by selected features and build outputs.
packages.security: Calls should be proxied or configured so client-side code never exposes service secrets.

packages.label: window.electronAPI
packages.responsibility: Renderer-side desktop capability detection and safe platform abstraction for file, shell, backup, updater, and notifications.
packages.security: Web code must guard Electron availability and never assume privileged APIs exist.

packages.label: Preload + IPC contract
packages.responsibility: Exposes a typed, limited API from Electron preload through contextBridge to the renderer.
packages.security: Context isolation and contract allowlists reduce renderer-to-main attack surface.

packages.label: Electron main process
packages.responsibility: Creates BrowserWindow, registers IPC handlers, manages CSP, auth handoff, shell, dialogs, updater, and app lifecycle.
packages.security: Window security, CSP, auth guard, and path validation protect privileged desktop operations.

packages.label: Desktop services
packages.responsibility: Auto updater, secure storage, biometric auth, encrypted auto backup, folder watcher, bid comparison, Python runner, and MCP server.
packages.security: IPC auth guard, allowed roots, encrypted backups, and URL/path checks are enforced here.

packages.label: Python Excel tools
packages.responsibility: Local or HTTP Excel merge/unlock helpers used by desktop and tool settings.
packages.security: File paths must be granted through desktop guards; HTTP unlock API requires an explicit header key.

packages.label: Node server + MCP
packages.responsibility: Express server, MCP endpoints, auth checks, rate limits, and structured MCP responses.
packages.security: Supabase auth, rate limiting, security headers, and response shaping protect remote calls.

packages.label: Excel tools API package
packages.responsibility: Node package for Excel workbook merge/unlock logic and CLI helpers.
packages.security: Workbook size limits and defensive formula/value handling reduce document-processing risk.

packages.label: Build scripts
packages.responsibility: Vite build, prerender, desktop compile/build, release prep, architecture checks, and manual assets.
packages.security: Build-time scripts should avoid secrets in logs and keep generated outputs separate from source.

packages.label: Vitest + checks
packages.responsibility: Unit, integration, security, boundary, and architecture tests.
packages.security: Security regressions are covered by dedicated tests for auth, RLS, IPC, CSP, markdown, Excel, and export paths.

flows.label: Invite new user
flows.summary: Owner/admin adds a teammate by email from organization member settings.
flows.risk: Medium: changes tenant membership and seat consumption.

steps.label: authenticated click
steps.detail: User opens settings through app shell.

steps.label: navigate
steps.detail: Route state selects settings view.

steps.label: lazy mount
steps.detail: Feature registry mounts Settings.

steps.label: member tab
steps.detail: OrganizationSettings renders OrgMembersTab.

steps.label: seat usage
steps.detail: OrgMembersTab loads seat usage before mutation.

steps.label: add by email
steps.detail: organizationService.addOrganizationMemberByEmail calls Supabase RPC.

steps.label: RPC
steps.detail: add_org_member_by_email validates caller role and writes membership.

steps.label: reload
steps.detail: Members, join requests, and seats reload after success.

steps.label: toast/modal
steps.detail: UIContext shows success or safe error.

flows.label: desktop build
flows.summary: Production Electron package build from web assets and desktop main process TypeScript.
flows.risk: Medium: packages privileged desktop app and release artifacts.

steps.label: terminal command
steps.detail: Developer invokes package script.

steps.label: Vite build
steps.detail: Vite compiles React app and prerender script prepares public output.

steps.label: desktop compile
steps.detail: desktop:compile runs TypeScript for Electron main/preload.

steps.label: preload bundle
steps.detail: Compiled preload exposes contextBridge API used by renderer.

steps.label: service wiring
steps.detail: Main process bundles updater, CSP, auth guard, secure storage, backup, and tool services.

steps.label: tool resources
steps.detail: Desktop package includes local Python helper folders where configured.

steps.label: recommended gates
steps.detail: Before merge/release run boundary, legacy, compile, and targeted tests.
steps.data: npm run check:boundaries, npm run check:legacy-structure

steps.label: electron-builder
steps.detail: electron-builder creates platform installer without publishing.

flows.label: Create project
flows.summary: User creates a project and opens it in the app.

steps.label: navigation
steps.detail: User opens project management from side navigation.

steps.label: route
steps.detail: Router updates URL and selected view.

steps.label: lazy mount
steps.detail: ProjectManager loads from feature registry.

steps.label: mutation
steps.detail: Project action delegates creation to app data/actions or project mutation hooks.

steps.label: service call
steps.detail: Project service maps UI model to persistence model.

steps.label: insert
steps.detail: Supabase stores project under owner/organization scope.

steps.label: invalidate
steps.detail: Project queries reload or optimistic state reconciles.

steps.label: open project
steps.detail: App navigates to project detail URL.

steps.label: ProjectLayout
steps.detail: Project detail view renders selected project.

flows.label: Import contacts
flows.summary: User imports subcontractor contacts from spreadsheet/CSV into tenant contacts.
flows.risk: Medium: imports user-provided file data and can bulk-update tenant contacts.

steps.label: open import
steps.detail: User starts ContactsImportWizard from Contacts/Settings.

steps.label: parse
steps.detail: contactsImportService parses rows and normalizes names/emails/specializations.

steps.label: validate model
steps.detail: Shared types and normalization rules keep contact shape consistent.

steps.label: preview merge
steps.detail: Wizard shows added/updated counts before commit.

steps.label: bulk mutation
steps.detail: Contacts state updates and invalidates contacts queries.

steps.label: persist
steps.detail: Contact service writes tenant-scoped contacts.

steps.label: upsert
steps.detail: Supabase/RLS stores contacts for current user or organization.

steps.label: refresh
steps.detail: Contacts view reloads latest data.

steps.label: result
steps.detail: User sees success/error summary.

flows.label: Update pipeline bid
flows.summary: User edits subcontractor/bid state inside project pipeline.
flows.risk: Medium: updates tender commercial state and contact status.

steps.label: project route
steps.detail: ProjectLayout receives selected project and active pipeline tab.

steps.label: render category
steps.detail: Pipeline UI renders category columns and bid cards.

steps.label: model rules
steps.detail: Bid status model and contact-name rules validate UI changes.

steps.label: onBidsChange
steps.detail: Card/modal sends updated bids to ProjectLayout actions.

steps.label: mutation/state
steps.detail: Project mutations update local state and schedule persistence.

steps.label: project service
steps.detail: Service serializes details for Supabase.

steps.label: update
steps.detail: Database update is protected by owner/org/editor policies.

steps.label: reconcile
steps.detail: Fresh state rerenders cards and comparison summaries.

flows.label: Generate contract protocol
flows.summary: Project contract module generates markdown/PDF-style protocol from selected contract and organization branding.

steps.label: contract action
steps.detail: User invokes protocol generation in project contract area.

steps.label: document registry
steps.detail: Contract document registry chooses template and status metadata.

steps.label: data load
steps.detail: Contract/document/project services collect contract details and organization metadata.

steps.label: read
steps.detail: Supabase returns authorized contract/project/branding data.

steps.label: markdown sanitize
steps.detail: Markdown rendering helpers limit unsafe output.

steps.label: preview
steps.detail: Project UI shows generated protocol summary/document panel.

steps.label: export
steps.detail: Export service can create downloadable document artifacts.

flows.label: Desktop auto backup
flows.summary: Desktop app periodically exports tenant/user data and writes encrypted local backups.
flows.entryAction: Desktop settings > backup enabled / scheduled time
flows.risk: High: reads tenant data and writes local encrypted files.

steps.label: settings API
steps.detail: Renderer calls window.electronAPI.backup through platform adapter.

steps.label: contextBridge
steps.detail: Preload invokes typed backup IPC channels.

steps.label: ipc handler
steps.detail: Main process validates channel and auth state.

steps.label: autoBackupService
steps.detail: Service schedules timer, exports data, encrypts AES-256-GCM, and writes backup file.

steps.label: export callback
steps.detail: Backup export obtains authorized JSON data from Supabase RPC/service callback.

steps.label: status
steps.detail: Result is returned to renderer and notification APIs can inform the user.

steps.label: visible status
steps.detail: UI shows backup state or actionable error.

flows.label: Shorten URL
flows.summary: User shortens links with Tender Flow short URLs or TinyURL fallback.
flows.risk: Medium: stores redirects and may call external service.

steps.label: navigate
steps.detail: URL shortener route is gated by feature manifest.

steps.label: feature gate
steps.detail: RequireFeature checks URL_SHORTENER capability.

steps.label: shortenUrl
steps.detail: urlShortenerService normalizes target URL and selects provider.

steps.label: tfurl write/read
steps.detail: Tender Flow provider stores code and target in short_urls.

steps.label: optional TinyURL
steps.detail: TinyURL provider is called through a function when configured.

steps.label: redirect lookup
steps.detail: ShortUrlRedirect resolves /s/:code and increments click count.

steps.label: safe result
steps.detail: UI receives short URL or normalized error.

flows.label: Voice assistant query
flows.summary: Desktop admin asks the assistant a question using current project/contact/contract context.
flows.risk: High: sends selected tenant context to AI APIs and can run local tools.

steps.label: availability
steps.detail: AppContent enables provider only for admin, desktop, authenticated, supported routes.

steps.label: context sources
steps.detail: Assistant receives projects, contacts, details, and contracts grouped by project.

steps.label: tool gateway
steps.detail: Tool gateway limits what the assistant can read or answer.

steps.label: AI request
steps.detail: Text/realtime API layer sends prompt and selected context.

steps.label: optional data reads
steps.detail: Existing authorized data services back additional lookups.

steps.label: desktop-only affordance
steps.detail: Assistant can be limited to desktop route/tool capabilities.

steps.label: response
steps.detail: Panel renders answer and tool results.

flows.label: Excel merge/unlock
flows.summary: User processes Excel files through desktop-native Python tools or server Excel API.
flows.risk: High: processes untrusted office documents and local files.

steps.label: provider resolve
steps.detail: Excel tools provider decides native desktop or HTTP provider.

steps.label: native path
steps.detail: Desktop renderer calls electronAPI when native provider is selected.

steps.label: IPC
steps.detail: Preload invokes python:runTool or python:mergeExcel.

steps.label: auth/path guard
steps.detail: IPC handler requires auth and allowed filesystem roots.

steps.label: python runner
steps.detail: PythonRunner selects merge or unlock script and executes it.

steps.label: process workbook
steps.detail: Python tool reads workbook and writes merged/unlocked output.

steps.label: HTTP/package path
steps.detail: HTTP provider or package can process workbook through Node Excel tools API.

steps.label: result
steps.detail: UI receives output file/blob and visible processing status.

flows.label: MCP OAuth consent
flows.summary: User grants MCP access through consent route and server-side auth checks.
flows.risk: High: authorizes external tooling access to account-scoped actions.

steps.label: consent route
steps.detail: Router detects /oauth/consent before normal app view rendering.

steps.label: auth context
steps.detail: Consent page runs inside authenticated AppContent context.

steps.label: consent service
steps.detail: mcpOAuthConsentService validates pending request details.

steps.label: session/RPC
steps.detail: Supabase auth and database verify the consenting user.

steps.label: MCP access
steps.detail: Server MCP endpoints later validate Supabase auth and rate limits.

steps.label: tool execution
steps.detail: MCP node handler maps validated requests to app data helpers.
